string.text:
A table showing how much damage this weapon does with each ammo type vs different body parts and armor values. 
Assumes 20m range, 100% weapon condition, and doesn't account for other factors which affect damage, such as barrel condition, silencers, artefacts, etc. 
 
%c[d_blue]Columns%c[default] : 
- %c[d_blue]Type%c[default]: Ammo type used for damage calculations on this row. 
- %c[d_blue]AP%c[default]: Armor penetration colored %c[dark_gray]grey%c[default] -> %c[d_yellow]yellow%c[default] by how well it punches through the listed armor values. 
- %c[d_blue]vs Torso X%c[default]: Damage vs torso with X armor. Colored %c[dark_gray]grey%c[default] -> %c[d_green]green%c[default] by how close it is to the max damage for this weapon+ammo combination. 
- %c[d_blue]vs Head X%c[default]: Same as torso, but for headshots (includes 3.65x damage bonus). 
 
%c[d_blue]Notes%c[default]: 
- Damage numbers are mainly a refletion of your opening salvo, as once an armor segment (head/torso+arms/legs) is broken, you will do near-full damage to that segment regardless of AP. 
- BRC values are just a more familiar way to represent the raw NPC armor values (which you can toggle on if you prefer). 
 
%c[d_blue]Math (20m range, current difficulty)%c[default]: 
1. range_factor = 1 + 0.1 x (k_air x 0.5 / (1 - k_air + 0.1)) 
2. combat_AP = k_ap x 10 (+ 0.04 on head) x tier_ap_scale / range_factor x difficulty x 0.80 
3. power = hit_power / range_factor x ammo_k_hit x bone_mult x tier_ap_scale x 1.1 x difficulty x pellets 
4. If combat_AP >= armor: damage = power x 100, else damage = power x chip_fraction x 100

Adds extra useful stats to the normal weapon stats section. 
 
%c[d_red]NEW STAT ROWS ADDED%c[default]: 
 
%c[d_green]Hit Power:%c[default] 
- The % of base ammo damage that this weapon inflicts (i.e. not all weapons that shoot 9mm do the same damage). 
 
%c[d_green]Damage Per Ammo:%c[default] 
- Raw damage per ammo type in table order (e.g. 45/38/22) without any other factors mixed in. 
 
%c[d_red]NEW VALUES APPENDED TO EXISTING ROWS%c[default]: 
 
%c[d_blue]Accuracy%c[default] + %c[d_green]Base Spread Degrees%c[default] 
- Standing ADS bullet dispersion in degrees (e.g. 0.35 deg means that final trajectory of the projectile can be up to 0.35 degrees off center). 
 
%c[d_blue]Handling%c[default] | %c[d_green]Movement Spread Degrees%c[default] 
- Hip-fire spread while walking and running (e.g. walk 2.1 deg / run 5.2 deg). This is added to base spread, but isn't applied while ADS. 
 
%c[d_blue]Reliability%c[default] | %c[d_green]Average Barrel Wear%c[default] 
- How much barrel condition is worn down per shot. Value is average of each ammo type with and without silencer. 
 
%c[d_blue]Recoil Control%c[default] | %c[d_green]Burst Control%c[default] 
- How many consecutive shots it takes to kick the cemera angle vertically by 3 degrees. 
 
%c[d_blue]Muzzle Velocity%c[default] | %c[d_green]Bullet Drop%c[default] 
- How far down a bullet will drop at 200m.

%c[d_blue]Silencers%c[default] are OP, they give you: 
- +7% damage and AP 
- +3% bullet speed 
- Better dispersion/recoil 
- x1.25 more weapon wear per shot. 
 
%c[d_blue]NPC body parts%c[default] take different amounts of damage: 
- Head: 365% 
- Jaw: 300% 
- Neck: 270% 
- Torso/Pelvis: 90% 
- Upper arms: 70% 
- Forearms: 60% 
- Thighs: 55% 
- Calves: 45% 
- Hands: 40% 
- Feet: 30% 
 
%c[d_blue]NPC ranks%c[default] very roughly align to these torso BRC values in the table: 
- Torso: 10 Novice, 20 Trainee, 30 Professional, 40 Veteran, 50 Master, 60 Legend 
 
%c[d_blue]Difficulty%c[default] multiplies damage and AP: 
- Stalker 130%, Veteran 105%, Master 90% 
 
%c[d_blue]NPC health%c[default] is one shared pool (not split across limbs). 
- Every stalker has the same max HP, but Sin take ~70% less damage, zombies ~40% less, ISG ~30% less, meaning they effectively have more health than other stalkers.
Adds rows to show Hit Power and raw Damage Per Ammo, plus extra stats on Accuracy, Handling, Reliability, Recoil, and Muzzle Velocity.
